mission.title: keycloak: Trusted Host don't set CORS headers in OIDC Client Registration
mission.description: Trusted Host don't set CORS headers in OIDC Client Registration. This issue affects 35+ users.

steps.title: Identify the CORS issue with OIDC Client Registration
steps.description:
Check if your Keycloak OIDC dynamic client registration endpoint is missing CORS headers:
```bash
# Test the CORS headers on the client registration endpoint:
curl -I -X OPTIONS \
  -H "Origin: https://your-app.example.com" \
  -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" \
  https://your-keycloak/realms/your-realm/clients-registrations/openid-connect
```
If CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin`) are missing in the response, this issue affects you.

steps.title: Understand the root cause
steps.description:
The `trusted-hosts` client policy in Keycloak's Client Registration controls which hosts are allowed to register clients. However, the trusted host policy was never intended to set CORS headers on the registration endpoint.

Setting `trusted-hosts` does NOT automatically add CORS headers for those hosts. CORS support for dynamic client registration is tracked as a separate feature request.

steps.title: Apply the workaround
steps.description:
Since the trusted-hosts policy does not control CORS, configure CORS at your reverse proxy or load balancer level:
1. If using a reverse proxy (nginx, HAProxy, etc.), add the appropriate CORS headers for the `/clients-registrations/` path
2. Alternatively, configure the Keycloak client's **Web Origins** setting to include the calling application's origin
3. Check the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40528) for the upstream fix that adds proper CORS support to the registration endpoint

steps.title: Verify CORS headers are present
steps.description:
After applying the fix (either via proxy configuration or upgrading to a Keycloak version with the fix):
```bash
curl -I -X OPTIONS \
  -H "Origin: https://your-app.example.com" \
  -H "Access-Control-Request-Method: POST" \
  https://your-keycloak/realms/your-realm/clients-registrations/openid-connect
```
Confirm that `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` is present in the response.

resolution.summary: The trusted-hosts client policy was never intended to affect CORS headers. CORS support for the OIDC dynamic client registration endpoint was added in a separate PR. Users on older versions can work around this by configuring CORS at the reverse proxy level.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with Admin Console access.
